fn to_seq(&self) -> (seq: AVLTreeSeqMtPerS<T>)
            requires self.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf(),
            ensures
                self@.finite(),
                seq@.to_set() =~= self@,
                seq.spec_avltreeseqmtper_wf(),
                forall|i: int| 0 <= i < seq@.len() ==> #[trigger] self@.contains(seq@[i]);

fn empty() -> (empty: Self)
            ensures empty@ == Set::<<T as View>::V>::empty(), empty.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf();

fn singleton(x: T) -> (tree: Self)
            ensures tree@ == Set::<<T as View>::V>::empty().insert(x@), tree@.finite(), tree.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf();

fn from_seq(seq: AVLTreeSeqMtPerS<T>) -> (constructed: Self)
            ensures constructed@.finite(), constructed.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf();

fn filter<F: PredMt<T> + Clone>(&self, f: F) -> (filtered: Self)

fn intersection(&self, other: &Self) -> (common: Self)
            ensures common@ == self@.intersect(other@), common@.finite(), common.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf();

fn difference(&self, other: &Self) -> (remaining: Self)
            ensures remaining@ == self@.difference(other@), remaining@.finite(), remaining.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf();

fn union(&self, other: &Self) -> (combined: Self)
            ensures combined@ == self@.union(other@), combined@.finite(), combined.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf();

fn delete(&self, x: &T) -> (updated: Self)
            ensures updated@ == self@.remove(x@), updated@.finite(), updated.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf();

fn insert(&self, x: T) -> (updated: Self)
            ensures updated@ == self@.insert(x@), updated@.finite(), updated.spec_avltreesetmtper_wf();
fn default() -> Self { Self::empty() }

proof fn zero_bit_false(i: u64)
        requires i < 64,
        ensures !get_bit64!(0u64, i),

proof fn set_bit64_proof(bv_new: u64, bv_old: u64, index: u64, bit: bool)
        requires
            bv_new == set_bit64!(bv_old, index, bit),
            index < 64,
        ensures
            get_bit64!(bv_new, index) == bit,
            forall|loc2: u64| #![auto] (loc2 < 64 && loc2 != index) ==>
                (get_bit64!(bv_new, loc2) == get_bit64!(bv_old, loc2)),

proof fn bit_or_64_proof(bv1: u64, bv2: u64, bv_new: u64)
        requires bv_new == bv1 | bv2,
        ensures forall|i: u64| #![auto] (i < 64) ==>
            get_bit64!(bv_new, i) == (get_bit64!(bv1, i) || get_bit64!(bv2, i)),

proof fn bit_and_64_proof(bv1: u64, bv2: u64, bv_new: u64)
        requires bv_new == bv1 & bv2,
        ensures forall|i: u64| #![auto] (i < 64) ==>
            get_bit64!(bv_new, i) == (get_bit64!(bv1, i) && get_bit64!(bv2, i)),

proof fn bit_andnot_64_proof(bv1: u64, bv2: u64, bv_new: u64)
        requires bv_new == bv1 & !bv2,
        ensures forall|i: u64| #![auto] (i < 64) ==>
            get_bit64!(bv_new, i) == (get_bit64!(bv1, i) && !get_bit64!(bv2, i)),

proof fn lemma_bounded_usize_set_finite(n: usize)
        ensures Set::new(|i: usize| (i as int) < n as int).finite()
        decreases n

proof fn lemma_view_finite(bits: Seq<u64>, universe_size: usize)
        requires bits.len() == num_words(universe_size as int),
        ensures Set::new(|i: usize|
            (i as int) < universe_size as int
            && u64_view(bits[i as int / 64])[i as int % 64]
        ).finite(),

fn singleton(u: usize, x: usize) -> (tree: Self)
            ensures
                (x < u ==> tree@ == Set::<usize>::empty().insert(x)),
                (x >= u ==> tree@ == Set::<usize>::empty()),
                tree@.finite(),
                tree.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                tree.spec_universe_size() == u;

fn filter<F: PredVal<usize> + Clone>(&self, f: F) -> (filtered: Self)
            requires
                self.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                forall|i: usize| i < self.spec_universe_size() ==> #[trigger] f.requires((i,)),
            ensures
                filtered@.finite(),
                filtered@.subset_of(self@),
                filtered.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                filtered.spec_universe_size() == self.spec_universe_size();

fn intersection(&self, other: &Self) -> (common: Self)
            requires
                self.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                other.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                self.spec_universe_size() == other.spec_universe_size(),
            ensures
                common@ == self@.intersect(other@),
                common@.finite(),
                common.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                common.spec_universe_size() == self.spec_universe_size();

fn difference(&self, other: &Self) -> (remaining: Self)
            requires

fn union(&self, other: &Self) -> (combined: Self)
            requires
                self.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                other.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                self.spec_universe_size() == other.spec_universe_size(),
            ensures

                combined@.finite(),
                combined.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                combined.spec_universe_size() == self.spec_universe_size();

fn delete(&mut self, x: usize)
            requires old(self).spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
            ensures
                self@ == old(self)@.remove(x),
                self@.finite(),
                self.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                self.spec_universe_size() == old(self).spec_universe_size();

fn insert(&mut self, x: usize)
            requires old(self).spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
            ensures
                (x < old(self).spec_universe_size() ==> self@ == old(self)@.insert(x)),
                (x >= old(self).spec_universe_size() ==> self@ == old(self)@),
                self@.finite(),
                self.spec_arraysetenummteph_wf(),
                self.spec_universe_size() == old(self).spec_universe_size();

fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> (equal: bool)

proof fn lemma_filter_remove<V>(s: Seq<V>, v: V)
        requires s.no_duplicates()
        ensures s.filter(|e: V| e != v).to_set() =~= s.to_set().remove(v)
        decreases s.len()

proof fn lemma_push_preserves_no_dups<V>(s: Seq<V>, x: V)
        requires
            s.no_duplicates(),
            !s.contains(x),
        ensures
            s.push(x).no_duplicates()

proof fn lemma_filter_in_original<V>(s: Seq<V>, pred: spec_fn(V) -> bool)
        ensures forall|v: V| s.filter(pred).contains(v) ==> #[trigger] s.contains(v)
        decreases s.len()

proof fn lemma_filter_preserves_no_dups<V>(s: Seq<V>, pred: spec_fn(V) -> bool)
        requires s.no_duplicates()
        ensures s.filter(pred).no_duplicates()
        decreases s.len()

proof fn lemma_filter_to_set_intersect<V>(s: Seq<V>, set: Set<V>)
        requires s.no_duplicates()
        ensures s.filter(|e: V| set.contains(e)).to_set() =~= s.to_set().intersect(set)
        decreases s.len()

proof fn lemma_filter_to_set_difference<V>(s: Seq<V>, set: Set<V>)
        requires s.no_duplicates()
        ensures s.filter(|e: V| !set.contains(e)).to_set() =~= s.to_set().difference(set)
        decreases s.len()

proof fn lemma_subseq_no_dups_subset<V>(orig: Seq<V>, sub: Seq<V>)
        requires
            orig.no_duplicates(),
            forall|i: int| #![trigger sub[i]] 0 <= i < sub.len() ==> orig.contains(sub[i]),
            sub.no_duplicates(),
        ensures
            sub.to_set().subset_of(orig.to_set())
